ddls:
CREATE TABLE "resource_allocations" (
  "allocation_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the resource allocation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the resource allocation */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of resource allocated (e.g., CPU, Memory) */,
  "allocated_time" REAL /* Time period for which the resource was allocated */,
  "resource_limit" REAL /* Maximum amount of resources that can be allocated */,
  "resource_used" REAL /* Actual amount of resources used by the allocation */,
  "allocation_status" TEXT /* Current status of the allocation (e.g., active, inactive, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allocation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each iteration of a task */,
  "allocation_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the resource allocation used */,
  "execution_cost" REAL /* Cost associated with the execution of the task */,
  "cycle_time" REAL /* Time taken for a single cycle of execution */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Total time taken to execute the task */,
  "cycle_time_deviation" REAL /* Deviation from the average cycle time */,
  "execution_time_deviation" REAL /* Deviation from the average execution time */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier of the task being executed */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task being executed */,
  "resource_utilization" REAL /* Percentage of resource utilization during task execution */,
  "execution_status" TEXT /* Status of the task execution (e.g., success, failure, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("iteration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_allocation_id FOREIGN KEY ("allocation_id") REFERENCES resource_allocations ("allocation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, admin, analyst) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who created the account */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the alert */,
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the iteration that generated the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., execution time exceeded) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., open, closed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_iteration_id FOREIGN KEY ("iteration_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("iteration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the historical record */,
  "iteration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the iteration */,
  "allocation_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the resource allocation used */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Total time taken to execute the task */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the data was recorded */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data being recorded (e.g., performance metrics, resource utilization) */,
  "data_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the data being recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_iteration_id FOREIGN KEY ("iteration_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("iteration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_historical_data_allocation_id FOREIGN KEY ("allocation_id") REFERENCES resource_allocations ("allocation_id")
)
